replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: Thank you so much for your beautiful words! Georgia really does feel almost unreal at times. I’m curious — which part felt the most fictional to you: the ancient mountain villages, the dramatic cliffs, or the way history is built into the landscape?

comments.author: @DataNanava-q5v
comments.text: As a Georgian I have visited 157 countries in the world and I've never seen this kind ppl except in Philippines and Japan they're so kind like us I didn't visited war countries also because it's dangerous for everyone and I wanted to say thank you for visiting our home country and making video about us I live in salkhino and its dangerous here too because everywhere are dangerous dogs in road one day I was coming back from school and when I was half done and still 850 meters were left I saw 7 huge dogs running straight to me from behind so I knew they were running to me to bite me so I ran so fast I think it was 39km/h that's highest I could run I was like moving car and we are weak for some reason but we still have more friends than enemies so we're not scared a lot were afraid of the day well lose our family and Russia attacks us so after war ends between Russia and Ukraine I'm gonna tell u to not visit our country for 1-2 years because they might attack us and if it happens I'm going back to Azerbaijan or somewhere because we know English only and we want to go in a country where they speak English and our mom dad or anyone except me my bros don't know English because back in their day there was no English learning and this is story from me and this is actually true not made up and I want to thank you again for visiting our country! ❤❤

comments.author: @bobbyking2490
comments.text: I am an American convert to Orthodoxy. Georgia (Sakartvelo) is a place I wish to visit someday! The Georgian chant is so incredibly beautiful. I especially love the hymns “as many as have been baptized” and “you are the vineyard” and “Holy God” ❤️☦️🇬🇪

comments.author: @codekop
comments.text:
I don’t even know how to express my gratitude for this video. As a Georgian, seeing our history presented with such care, respect, and attention to detail means a great deal to me. Thank you for taking the time to create and share this with the world. You are helping people learn about a history that deserves to be known.

I would love to share this video with others, if I may, so that more people can discover and appreciate Georgia’s rich history. Truly outstanding work.

comments.author: @RodYates
comments.text: I wish more "westerners" would watch videos such as this. We forget that there are parts of the world where US  nonsence doesn't overshadow daily life. In these places people seem to just LIVE.  The substance of simply dealing with ordinary existence occupies their daily lives, and time probably works in different ways.

comments.author: @Indonesiasedangberk
comments.text: Georgia’s culture is as rich as its landscapes—filled with heartfelt hospitality, timeless traditions, and unforgettable flavors. It is a place where every meal feels like a celebration, every view feels like a painting, and every moment feels meaningful.
